title: Write a correct incremental dbt model
description: Steps to define the incremental predicate, late-arriving window, and idempotent merge so it matches a full refresh.
body: Use when converting a costly full-refresh model to incremental. Do not append rows where the timestamp simply exceeds the last run — backdated and updated rows are then missed. Actions: configure incremental with a unique key for merge/upsert, set the filter to a lookback window covering late arrivals, handle updates via merge, and reconcile the result against a full refresh. Validation signals include an incremental build matching a full refresh on the same input and a reconciliation diff within tolerance. Risk boundary: a too-narrow lookback drops late data and the model silently undercounts; if reconciliation drifts, treat the model as incorrect, full-refresh it, and widen the window before relying on it.
